summaryText: Pastor Darren Tellez, in his final sermon at Impact Church before planting a church in Miami, delivered a powerful message on enduring the gap between receiving a God-given dream and seeing it fulfilled. He opened by honoring Pastor Travis Hearn and reading from Acts 2, emphasizing that God is pouring out His Spirit and stirring up God-sized dreams in His people. The core of the message centered on the life of Joseph, illustrating that a divine call is always accompanied by a season of time, which is often a season of suffering. Pastor Darren taught that the call of God costs more than we budget for, requiring us to be qualified through preparation and suffering. He explained that suffering is the currency that covers the cost of our calling, and it is an invitation to know Christ more deeply, both in the power of His resurrection and in sharing in His sufferings. The sermon provided two key lessons on how to 'suffer well': suffering confidently, by holding onto the certainty of God's call, and suffering patiently, by trusting God's timing in the 'weight room' of waiting. Pastor Darren vulnerably shared his family's experience of losing a son, testifying that the season of suffering ultimately produces strength and is always worth the wait. He concluded with an altar call for those in seasons of suffering and for salvation, encouraging everyone to give God glory in the midst of their trials.
mainPoints: A God-given dream always comes with a season of time, which is often a season of preparation and suffering.
To suffer well, we must suffer confidently, holding onto the certainty of God's call despite our circumstances.
To suffer well, we must also suffer patiently, trusting God's timing in the 'weight room' of waiting, which builds spiritual strength.

versesMentioned.text: 'In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy, your young men will see visions, your old men will dream dreams.'
Summary of Joseph's dreams and his family's reaction, as detailed in the sermon narrative.
'For many are invited, but few are chosen.'
'I want to know Christ—yes, to know the power of his resurrection and participation in his sufferings, becoming like him in his death,'
'I lift up my eyes to the mountains—where does my help come from? My help comes from the LORD, the Maker of heaven and earth.'
'but those who hope in the LORD will renew their strength. They will soar on wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary, they will walk and not be faint.'
Summary of Joseph revealing his identity to his brothers and declaring that God intended his suffering for a greater purpose.
'And the God of all grace, who called you to his eternal glory in Christ, after you have suffered a little while, will himself restore you and make you strong, firm and steadfast.'

Monday.scripture: Acts 2:17 - 'In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people...'
Monday.action: Spend 5 minutes in prayer, asking God to stir or clarify a God-sized dream in your heart.
Monday.prayer: Lord, pour out Your Spirit on me. Stir up the dreams You have for my life and give me faith to believe for them.

Tuesday.scripture: Philippians 3:10 - 'I want to know Christ...and participation in his sufferings...'
Tuesday.focus: Understanding the cost of the call.
Tuesday.action: Identify one area where following God's call is currently costing you (e.g., comfort, a relationship, stability). Offer it to God in prayer.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, I want to know You more. Help me accept the cost of my calling as an invitation into deeper fellowship with You.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 121:1-2 - 'My help comes from the LORD...'
Wednesday.action: When a challenge arises today, pause and declare out loud: 'My help comes from the Lord, the Maker of heaven and earth.'
Wednesday.prayer: God, when I feel insecure or attacked, remind me that You have called me. My confidence is in You alone.

Thursday.scripture: Isaiah 40:31 - '...those who hope in the LORD will renew their strength...'
Thursday.action: Choose to wait patiently on one thing today without complaining or forcing a solution. Trust God's timing.
Thursday.prayer: Father, in this season of waiting, build my spiritual stamina. Teach me to hope in You and receive Your strength.

Friday.scripture: Genesis 45:5 - '...it was to save lives that God sent me ahead of you.'
Friday.focus: Seeing God's purpose in past pain.
Friday.action: Reflect on a past difficulty. Write down one way God used it for good or how it prepared you for something later.
Friday.prayer: God, help me see my past sufferings through Your eyes. Thank You for working all things together for good.

Saturday.scripture: 1 Peter 5:10 - '...after you have suffered a little while...will himself restore you...'
Saturday.action: Praise God in advance for His restoration in an area where you are currently suffering or waiting.
Saturday.prayer: God of all grace, I praise You that my suffering is not forever. I trust You to restore, strengthen, and establish me. It is worth the wait.
